paper_title: Erosion in the coastal areas of the Vietnamese Mekong Delta: Current challenges and solutions

entities.description: Corresponding author from the College of Environment and Natural Resources.

entities.id: College of Environment and Natural Resources, Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic institution in Vietnam where the author is affiliated.
The research field focusing on coastal erosion and management strategies in the Mekong Delta.
The primary geological disaster and research problem occurring in the Mekong Delta.
The assessment of long-term effectiveness of engineering works against erosion.
Practical research approach conducted from 2021 to 2023 along coastal provinces.
A stability formula used for the design and calculation of armor units in breakwaters.
A low-cost method using two layers of bamboo or Melaleuca poles to dissipate wave energy.
Tube-shaped geotextile bags filled with sand used as wave-blocking underground dikes.
A structural solution involving concrete piles to reduce wave energy and restore mangrove forests.
A 4-legged concrete coastal breakwater unit designed to block and break waves.

entities.id: National Steering Committee for Natural Disaster Prevention and Control website
entities.description: The source of statistics and records regarding landslide spots and lengths in Vietnam.
The estimated total area of coastal land lost each year in the Mekong Delta.
The approximate implementation cost for constructing a bamboo/Melaleuca fence.
The average construction cost for prestressed centrifugal concrete revetments.
The effectiveness of the centrifugal concrete embankment in reducing wave energy.

relations.source: Erosion in the coastal areas of the Vietnamese Mekong Delta: Current challenges and solutions
